content.full_text:
March 20, 1992
P.O.Box 720594
San Jose, Cal. 95172

Blanca Breeze
legal department
Fair Political Practices
Sacramento, Cal.

Dear Blanca:

As per my calls of March 18th and today, I am herewith
requesting a written decision in my case put before you over
the phone onthe 18th,.

I created a controlled committee on December 19th 1991 to
prepare for my candidacy in a Community college Trustee
Electien, to be held this November for which I would file in
August.

On March 3, I filed and qualified for the Greens County
Central committee. And as only seven people filed for the
available seven seats I stand to be elected June 2.

I have since been informed, by a vocal member of the
committee elect, that as they intend to file a non-controled
committee under the Central Committee, I can not sit on the
Central committee and run for Trustee Board, as that would
cause a conflict of interest - vis a vis being a member for a
time of both the controlled and noncontroled committee
(Letter enclosed)

I understand by our conversation, today that is not the
case and there is no problem or conflict according to State
Fair Political Practices, and I respectfully request you send
that decision to me in writing.

Sincerely,

Tim K.Fitzgerald

CC: Jim Stauffer
Kyle Burke

Enclosures


GREEN PARTY
OF SANTA CLARA COUNTY

March 9, 1992

TO: Kyle Burke, San Jose Local

FROM: Jim Stauffer

SUBJECT: County Council participation of Tim Fitzgerald

There has been expressed concern over Tim's election to the
County Council (Central Committee) as the representative from the
San Jose Local. The primary concern is the affect his campaign
for an elected office will have on the Council's legal status and
financial reporting requirements. Per our conversation on March
6, this information is being provided to you as a request for the
San Jose Local to determine if Tim should continue as your
Council representative.

There are two types of Committees. A controlled committee is
formed around a candidate for office, a ballot measure or
initiative. A Committee that is not controlled is formed to
engage in general political activities. The Greens, as well as
most other Central Committees, have chosen to form a not-
controlled Committee. As such, no member of our Council is
allowed to run for an elective office.

Tim was informed of this apparent conflict and requested to
consult with the PPPC. His response to me that no conflict
existed since he had formed a separate controlled committee for
his campaign. I also contacted the PPPC and received a
conflicting response - i.e.
contradicting response. I explained the specific situation - i.e
a Committee member running for an elective position on a school
board that had formed a separate committee for his campaign. The
board rep said unequivocally a conflict still existed. The
PPPC rep said unequivocally a conflict would have to
exist if the candidate's participation on the Central Committee would have to
be such that he or she "ceremonial" during his campaign.
When pressed for more clarification, the PPPC rep said his
participation would have to be "ceremonial" during his campaign.
I must interpret this to mean that Tim would not be allowed to
participate in decision making, organizing, or advocating
positions or issues. Given that our Council is also going to
function as the county's coordinating committee, Tim would be
rendered ineffective in this capacity, so the appropriateness of
his being on this Council must be questioned.

[This image appears to be a mostly blank or very faded document with minimal visible text. There are some faint horizontal lines visible in the middle and lower portions of the page, but the text is not legible enough to transcribe accurately.]
